cards.text: A quadratic equation in the variable x is of the form ax2 + bx + c = 0, where a, b, c are real numbers and a {tex}\neq{/tex} 0.

flash_cards.topic_name: Solution of a Quadratic Equation by Factorisation (X-4.2)

cards.text: If we can factorise ax2 + bx + c, a {tex}\neq{/tex} 0, into a product of two linear factors, then the roots of the quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0 can be found by equating each factor to zero.
cards.topic_name: Solution of a Quadratic Equation by Factorisation (X-4.2)

cards.text: A real number {tex}\alpha{/tex} is said to be a root of the quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0, if a{tex}\alpha{/tex}2 + b{tex}\alpha{/tex} + c = 0. The zeroes of the quadratic polynomial ax2 + bx + c and the roots of the quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0 are the same.
cards.topic_name: Solution of a Quadratic Equation by Factorisation (X-4.2)

cards.text:
A quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0 has

  1. two distinct real roots, if b2 - 4ac > 0,
  2. two equal roots (i.e., coincident roots), if b2 - 4ac = 0, and
  3. no real roots, if b2 - 4ac < 0.
Quadratic formula: The roots of a quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0 are given by {tex}\frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4 a c}}{2 a}{/tex}, provided b2 - 4ac {tex}\geq{/tex} 0.

Ovary (2n) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Fruit
